concerns.text: Constitution Principle II (Verified Accuracy) claims Reference-Validator Agent runs on artifact write, but the plan structure shows no code file or task reference for this agent integration. The plan structure lists code/download/, code/compute/, code/analyze/, code/reproducibility/, and code/main.py - none reference the Reference-Validator Agent workflow required by Principle II.

CompositeComplexityScore entity is defined in data-model.md with attributes (score_id, crossing_weight, braid_weight, per_knot_scores, pearson_correlation, spearman_correlation, effect_size, validation_sample_size, random_seed) but has no corresponding contract schema file. All other entities (KnotRecord, InvariantsDataset, RegressionModel) have matching contract schemas; this is a contract coverage gap.

Project structure lists only 2 contracts (knot_record.schema.yaml, regression_output.schema.yaml) but 6 contract files exist (knot_record, knot_data, invariants_dataset, regression_model, regression_output, regression_result). The plan documentation does not match the actual contract inventory, creating ambiguity about which schemas are canonical.

Contract test section lists test_knot_record_schema.py and test_regression_output_schema.py but 6 contract files exist. There should be 6 corresponding contract test files to ensure all schemas are exercised, per the 'every contract has at least one plan element that exercises it' requirement.

Data model defines 'RegressionModel' entity but plan shows 'regression_outputs.json' as output file name. Contract files have three regression schemas (regression_model, regression_output, regression_result) with inconsistent field naming (vif_values vs vif_scores). This creates ambiguity about which contract schema governs regression output.

Constitution Principle V (Versioning Discipline) mentions content hashes and updated_at timestamp in state YAML, but the plan data flow does not explicitly show the state/projects/PROJ-552-quantifying-the-complexity-of-knot-diagr.yaml file as part of the transformation pipeline. This breaks the SSoT traceability chain.
